summary: Drew DeVault's March 2019 status update covers a productive period of full-time free software work. The headline achievement is the release of Sway 1.0, alongside progress on touch support for the Librem 5, a Wayland-Godot integration project (gdwlroots), and the aerc2 email client rewrite in Go. He also details RISC-V hardware tinkering for builds.sr.ht, contributions to Alpine Linux including Python 2 removal planning, and ongoing SourceHut improvements including Mercurial support.
key_points: Sway 1.0 was released and touch support work began for the Librem 5 devkit running postmarketOS
A gdwlroots project integrates wlroots with Godot, with a long-term vision for VR compositors and 3D Wayland standards
The aerc2 email client rewrite from C to Go is progressing well, leveraging Go's concurrency model for async design
RISC-V hardware work continues toward builds.sr.ht integration, including creative hardware hacking with relays and microSD isolation
Significant Alpine Linux contributions including Python package overhaul planning and Python 2 removal
money_quotes: My todo list is getting completed at a pace it's never seen before, and growing at a new pace, too.
This medium doesn't depend on a proprietary service, allows for richer content, and is useful for my supporters who support my work via other donation platforms.
The long-term goal is to build a VR compositor based on Godot and develop standards for Wayland applications to have 3D content.
I'm glad I chose to rewrite it from C, though my love for C still runs deep.

aerc2's progress is a great example of my marginalized projects becoming my side projects, as my side projects become my full-time job, and thus all of them are developing at a substantially improved pace.
I don't want to run untrusted builds.sr.ht code when that microSD card is plugged in.
key_insight: Full-time free software work creates a virtuous cycle where marginalized projects become side projects and side projects become full-time work, dramatically accelerating development across an entire ecosystem.
